name: align_exceeds_input
reason: Alignment rounding down should never produce a value exceeding the original input
output_tail:

name: align_not_multiple
reason: The whole purpose of align_to_usize is to produce a multiple of b; failing this means alignment is broken
output_tail:
ultiple(a: usize, b: usize)
    |       ----------------------------------------------- at the end of the function body
...
691 |         align_to_usize(a, b) % b != 0,
    |         ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ failed this postcondition

name: align_changes_already_aligned
reason: An already-aligned value should be a fixed point; changing it means the function over-rounds
output_tail:
gned(a: usize, b: usize)
    |       ---------------------------------------------------------- at the end of the function body
...
691 |         align_to_usize(a, b) != a,
    |         ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ failed this postcondition

name: align_not_idempotent
reason: Alignment must be idempotent; applying it twice should yield the same result as once
output_tail:
------------------- at the end of the function body

691 |         align_to_usize(align_to_usize(a, b), b) != align_to_usize(a, b),

error: aborting due to 1 previous error; 1 warning emitted

name: align_distance_exceeds_modulus
reason: The gap between the input and its aligned value must be strictly less than b; a gap >= b means an entire alignment block was skipped
